records.morph: [imp. Undertook (?); p. p. Undertaken (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Undertaking.]

defs: To take upon one's self; to engage in; to enter upon; to take in hand; to begin to perform; to set about; to attempt.
quotes: To second, or oppose, or undertake The perilous attempt.

defs: Specifically, to take upon one's self solemnly or expressly; to lay one's self under obligation, or to enter into stipulations, to perform or to execute; to covenant; to contract.
quotes: I 'll undertake to land them on our coast.

defs: Hence, to guarantee; to promise; to affirm.
quotes: And he was not right fat, I undertake.
And those two counties I will undertake Your grace shall well and quietly enjoiy.
I dare undertake they will not lose their labor.
It is not fit your lordship should undertake every companion that you give offense to.

defs: To have knowledge of; to hear.
To take or have the charge of.
quotes: Keep well those that ye undertake.

defs: To take upon one's self, or assume, any business, duty, or province.
quotes: O Lord, I am oppressed; undertake for me.
It is the cowish terror of his spirit That dare not undertake.

defs: To give a promise or guarantee; to be surety.
quotes: But on mine honor dare I undertake For good lord Titus' innocence in all.
